elements.section-2411-005: Indices in higher dimensions and the Poincar\'e problem
elements.sent-2411-447: As noted before, indices and residues play a significant role for the study of the famous Poincaré problem in dimensions 2 and higher.
elements.sent-2411-448: In this section we review some of these.
elements.sent-2411-449: The literature is vast and we will focus only on some of the works most related with this article.
elements.sent-2411-450: Let us start by saying a few words about the problem itself.
elements.sent-2411-452: Poincaré asked whether it is possible to decide if an algebraic differential equation in two variables is algebraically integrable, and he observed that in order to answer this question it is sufficient to know whether one can bound the degree of the generic leaves.
elements.sent-2411-453: He posed a question that in modern language can be stated as follows.
elements.sent-2411-454: Let $\F$ be a holomorphic foliation in $\mathbb P^2_{\C}$ and $C$ an irreducible curve which is invariant by $\F$: Is it possible to bound the degree of $C$ in terms of the degree of $\F$? This problem has been studied by many authors in more generality.
elements.sent-2411-455: One may ask, for instance, if given a foliation $\F$ on $\mathbb P^n_{\C}$ of degree $d$ and a hypersurface $V$ in $\mathbb P^n_{\C}$ invariant by $\F$, is it possible to bound the degree of $V$ by a number which depends only on $d$? The answer in general is obviously negative, even for $n=2$.
